bounded_streaming_memory_gate.claim_boundary: The CLI now has a conservative target-table estimate preflight for v2 compression plus decompression output/intermediate-layer allocation guards for v1/v2 files. Indexed and streaming v2 also have explicit experimental chunked target-table paths verified on deterministic fixtures. This is not full RSS containment, not natural-corpus proof, and not production readiness.
bounded_streaming_memory_gate.stop_rule: Do not extend planted scale, broaden search, or market bounded-memory operation until chunked target tables are verified on promoted workloads with full RSS evidence.

experiment_queue.conclusion: No ready ungated experiments remain; do not spend on gated compute or production GPU until evidence improves.

external_corpus_accession.next_allowed_action: human review may decide whether prefix-audit stage should be proposed
external_corpus_accession.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; accession validation only; not a compression claim; not natural-corpus proof; does not override SEARCH_FRONTIER_GATE.

frozen_rank_coded_span_generator.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; manifest/spec/golden-vector only; not a compression claim; not natural-corpus proof; not `.tlmr` format support.
frozen_rank_coded_span_generator.next_allowed_action: request human review of the frozen-rank accession/control contract before any replay
frozen_rank_coded_span_generator.conclusion: The rank-coded lane now has a frozen contract and golden vectors, but it remains blocked until human review authorizes replay. No compute, seed search, or compression claim is authorized by this artifact.

frozen_rank_source_candidates.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; source-candidate requirements only; not corpus accession; not natural-corpus proof; not `.tlmr` format support.
frozen_rank_source_candidates.next_allowed_action: select externally licensed source URIs and create payload manifest rows with paired controls, then rerun external accession validation
frozen_rank_source_candidates.conclusion: The frozen-rank lane now has an acquisition matrix, but no source candidate is ready for the external manifest or replay.

lattice_selection_heldout_probe.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; exact candidate replay only; selector utility probe; not `.tlmr` format support; not natural-corpus proof.
lattice_selection_heldout_probe.conclusion: Deterministic superposition fixtures prove selector auditability, but current non-planted held-out candidate sets show zero selected-byte improvement over greedy selection.

long_span_bundle_gate.stop_rule: Do not run broad long-span bundle sweeps until search frontier, raw-suffix break-even, selected-span, and control gates pass.
long_span_bundle_gate.conclusion: Current evidence blocks broad long-span bundle sweeps.

mechanism_closure_audit.top_blocked_next_action: Treat this as the top blocked gate; do not run broad long-span sweeps.
mechanism_closure_audit.forbidden_work: No broad raw depth search, long-span sweeps, format promotion, public preset promotion, sidecar packing, or production GPU work until generated gates move.
mechanism_closure_audit.conclusion: All current bounded mechanism lanes are closed, blocked, or maintenance-only; the next scientific move must be a materially new byte-to-seed mechanism or a stronger reversible frontier generator.

mechanism_experiment_ranking.conclusion: Run seed-table preset probing before raw-depth escalation, format promotion, descriptor packing, or hardware acceleration.

natural_corpus_reopen_manifest.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; manifest/pre-registration only; not a compression claim; not natural-corpus proof; does not override SEARCH_FRONTIER_GATE.
natural_corpus_reopen_manifest.conclusion: Natural-corpus viability remains blocked. The only currently aligned work is manifest-only corpus/control pre-registration; every compute stage remains closed until new generated evidence changes the gates.

next_mechanism_designs.allowed_next_work: design review, manifest work, golden vectors, and tiny deterministic proof harnesses only
next_mechanism_designs.forbidden_next_work: No broad seed search, no long-span sweep, no format promotion, no public preset registry, and no production acceleration claim.
next_mechanism_designs.conclusion: The next viable research move is not compute; it is to choose and specify one materially new byte-to-seed mechanism with controls strong enough to reopen generated gates.

public_preset_ablation_projection.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; proportional projection only; not exact decode proof; not public preset promotion; not `.tlmr` support.
public_preset_ablation_projection.conclusion: Existing rows suggest project/common-token removal may leave several ordinary wins, and proportional same-family removal would clear the current paired-shadow controls while preserving ordinary projected wins. This is a strong reason to run an exact bounded rerun, not a promotion claim.

public_preset_control_ablation.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; ablation manifest only; not public preset promotion; not `.tlmr` format support; not natural-corpus proof.
public_preset_control_ablation.conclusion: Public preset controls need paired-shadow expansion, dictionary-size equalization, project-token removal, leave-family-out policy, and density normalization before promotion can reopen.

public_preset_control_audit.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; not public preset promotion; not `.tlmr` format support; not natural-corpus proof.
public_preset_control_audit.conclusion: The public-preset signal is promising but currently falsified for promotion by paired shadow controls.

public_preset_control_rerun.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; exact dictionary rerun only; not public preset promotion; not `.tlmr` format support; not natural-corpus proof.
public_preset_control_rerun.conclusion: The bounded exact rerun blocks promotion: project-token removal keeps paired-shadow controls negative, leave-family-out clears controls but falls below the three-group ordinary floor, and leave-family-out plus project-token removal removes the signal.

public_preset_promotion_gate.stop_rule: Do not add a public preset registry or .tlmr preset metadata until ordinary held-out diversity, control separation, random-trial baseline, exact-decode, and SEARCH_FRONTIER_GATE format-promotion gates all pass.
public_preset_promotion_gate.conclusion: Public presets are the strongest non-depth mechanism signal, but current controls block promotion.

recursive_structured_fixtures.stop_reason: fewer than two ordinary structured fixture families have later-layer wins
recursive_structured_fixtures.conclusion: Recursive v2 later-layer gains remain unpromoted outside planted/alignment controls.

scale_performance_report.stop_rule: Do not extend planted-density scale runs unless peak/estimated-table ratio stays near the observed plateau or chunked target tables reduce the working set.
scale_performance_report.conclusion: Current 16 MiB planted-density scaling is explainable but memory-heavy.

schema_native_public_dictionary_replicat.conclusion: Schema-native public dictionary replication does not yet survive the harder control gate.

search_frontier_gate.next_action: Design a materially new byte-to-seed mechanism or stronger reversible frontier generator; do not spend on broad raw depth search from the current prefix-only frontier.

seed_table_fasta_ablation.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; bounded FASTA attribution ablation only; not natural-corpus proof; not production proof; not .tlmr format support.
seed_table_fasta_ablation.conclusion: The sequence lane can reopen only if header-scrubbed and sequence-only FASTA rows stay negative while controls remain null and selected spans are mostly sequence-attributed.

seed_table_preset_probe.conclusion: Canonical seed-table v0 does not meet the promotion gate; treat this as evidence for the next mechanism decision, not format support.

seed_table_preset_replay.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; bounded preset replay only; not natural-corpus proof; not production proof; not .tlmr format support.
seed_table_preset_replay.conclusion: Replay can reopen the seed-table lane only if unrelated ordinary negative groups appear while controls remain null. The artifact is evidence for mechanism triage, not a compression or format claim.

streaming_economics_gate.claim_boundary: Streaming is the scalable CPU matcher path and planted-scale evidence, not natural-corpus proof, not production proof, and not a search-frontier override.
streaming_economics_gate.stop_rule: Do not widen seed depth, GPU work, or format promotion from streaming economics alone; require non-planted selected spans or negative rows with controls intact and SEARCH_FRONTIER_GATE reopened.

superposition_telemetry.conclusion: Candidate-lattice telemetry satisfies the deterministic selector gate.

ui_workflow_smoke.stop_rule: Do not call the desktop workflow covered unless this static schema smoke and the Tauri command tests stay green.
ui_workflow_smoke.conclusion: The evidence ledger panel and Tauri research-artifact DTO are statically aligned.

whole_stream_residual_vector_probe.stop_reason: frozen packed replication has zero ordinary held-out negative groups; whole-stream vector rows do not reach three unrelated ordinary groups
whole_stream_residual_vector_probe.conclusion: Whole-stream residual vectors do not yet turn the residual sidecar family into broad compression evidence.

summary.claim_boundary: No Seed Search; not natural-corpus proof; not production proof; not a compression claim.
summary.conclusion: Keep exploring bounded mechanism hypotheses, but do not reopen broad compute or production promotion until generated gates move.

hypotheses.title: Public seed-table presets can reshape the byte-to-seed distribution
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: table lookup from a seed table / public Lotus preset
hypotheses.best_case_argument: A frozen decoder-public codebook could make the generative space less cryptographically random for structured corpora while staying lossless and avoiding file-local training leakage.
hypotheses.current_evidence: canonical selected spans=37768, public gate qualified=6/8, blocked gates=['control-separation', 'format-promotion-boundary']; control audit status=blocked_by_paired_shadow_controls with strongest control shadow-openapi-control; ablations required=4; projected leave-family-out ordinary groups=9 and control groups=0; exact rerun status=exact_rerun_blocks_public_preset_promotion with no-project control groups=2, leave-family-out ordinary groups=2, and clean no-project leave-family-out ordinary groups=0. bounded replay found ordinary negative groups=1, control negative groups=0, selected spans=4577, promotion candidate=False. FASTA ablation header artifact likely=True, sequence lane reopen candidate=False, total header-selected spans=480, total sequence-selected spans=0, stop reasons=['win_disappears_after_header_scrubbing', 'header_only_control_negative', 'control_negative_group_present']. Mechanism closure status=no_ready_mechanism_compute with ready compute lanes=0 and top blocked lane=long-span-bundle-gate.
hypotheses.falsification_test: The current standards preset is falsified by exact rerun; any successor must freeze discovery/held-out splits and show ordinary held-out negative groups survive while controls stay zero.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: At least three ordinary held-out negative groups, zero control negative groups, exact decode from preset/version metadata only, and improvement beyond equivalent random-trial scaling.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not add seed-table preset metadata to .tlmr until generated promotion gates pass and shadow controls stay null.

hypotheses.title: Frozen rank-coded span generation can make seed expansion corpus-shaped
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: table lookup from a seed table, but rank-coded and external-provenance first
hypotheses.best_case_argument: A decoder-public rank table could make seed bytes name common external spans directly, giving the compressor a non-uniform generative prior without storing per-file raw data or widening raw hash depth.
hypotheses.current_evidence: contract status=blocked_waiting_for_human_review; golden vectors=4; external manifest ready=True; paired manifest ready=True; compute allowed=False; replay allowed=False; promotion ready=False. source candidate status=candidate_matrix_only; candidate families=4; required manifest rows before replay=16; source candidates ready for manifest=0; source candidates ready for replay=0.
hypotheses.falsification_test: The rank table cannot be frozen from external provenance, controls go negative, or held-out gains are no better than same-size random rank tables and flat dictionary baselines.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: An external rank-table manifest is complete, at least three unrelated ordinary held-out groups produce selected exact spans and full-stream negative rows after selector/version metadata, and paired shadow, same-size random, wrong-family, binary, and high-entropy controls stay non-negative.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Keep the fixture table out of .tlmr and do not run held-out replay until external accession and paired controls are ready.

hypotheses.title: Streaming stratified target tiers can make wider search economically testable
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: stratified block tables plus one seed expansion checked across many tiers
hypotheses.best_case_argument: The serious engine should expand each seed once, compare generated prefixes against multiple equal-length target tiers, and verify hits without hashing target spans.
hypotheses.current_evidence: streaming gate status=search_closed_streaming_maintenance, bounded memory status=chunked_target_table_fixture_evidence_no_rss, target-table preflight=True, full RSS containment=False, indexed/streaming parity=True, streaming control negatives=0, ordinary non-planted streaming negatives=0, compute reopen allowed=False; search frontier selected spans=0, broad_depth_search_allowed=False, 16 MiB planted scale peak memory=1934.602 MiB.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Show streaming/indexed parity on fixtures while target-table memory or seed-expansion throughput fails to improve enough to justify larger runs.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: A generated scale artifact shows bounded memory growth, exact parity, and repeatable non-planted selected spans before raw-depth expansion.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not widen search solely because streaming exists; reopen compute only when search-frontier gates move.

hypotheses.title: Arity and phase search may expose longer profitable spans
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: continue to search farther and match higher-arity blocks
hypotheses.best_case_argument: Arity gives each discovered seed more bites at the apple by letting one record cover longer spans, especially if candidate starts are not locked to an arbitrary block grid.
hypotheses.current_evidence: long-span gate met 0/8; positive alignment exact hits=0; selected spans=0.

hypotheses.promotion_trigger: Generated alignment/arity artifacts show repeatable exact selected spans on ordinary held-out corpora while random and binary controls stay null.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not run broad long-span bundle sweeps while the long-span bundle gate and search frontier remain closed.

hypotheses.title: A deterministic candidate lattice can preserve useful alternatives before selection
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: superposition of possible replacements before collapse to selected records
hypotheses.best_case_argument: Keeping near-best candidates temporarily can prevent greedy local choices from destroying a better global bundle, while decompression still sees only ordinary selected v1/v2 records.
hypotheses.current_evidence: weighted beats greedy fixtures=1, weighted extra savings=4, promotion_met=True; held-out replay rows=220, weighted extra positive rows=0, ordinary extra groups=0, promotion_met=False. This is selector correctness, not compression utility proof.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Current held-out candidate replay found zero weighted-extra rows; future retained alternatives must improve selected savings on held-out ordinary rows after literal and metadata overhead.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: Candidate-lattice telemetry improves selected bytes on non-planted held-out rows without requiring recursive superposition or decoder state.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Disallow recursive superposition; recursive passes operate only on already selected layer outputs.

hypotheses.title: Reversible preconditioners may move near misses into exact seed spans
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: transform the byte landscape so later seed search has new opportunities
hypotheses.best_case_argument: A reversible transform could expose a corpus channel that finite seed outputs cover better than raw bytes, as long as metadata and inverse decode are charged honestly.
hypotheses.current_evidence: natural held-out prefix5 rows=0, exact hits=0, selected spans=0; prefix-4 movement has not promoted.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Transforms keep producing prefix-only movement or transform-only byte shortening without exact seed-span wins after metadata.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: A generated transform artifact reports exact selected seed spans on held-out ordinary corpora with transform metadata charged.

hypotheses.dispatch_prompt:
Hypothesis reversible-transform-preconditioners: use dispatching-parallel-agents as the operating model.
Best case: A reversible transform could expose a corpus channel that finite seed outputs cover better than raw bytes, as long as metadata and inverse decode are charged honestly.
Whitepaper concept: transform the byte landscape so later seed search has new opportunities
Current evidence: natural held-out prefix5 rows=0, exact hits=0, selected spans=0; prefix-4 movement has not promoted.
Scope: No Seed Search, not a compression claim, not natural-corpus proof, and not production proof.
Falsification test: Transforms keep producing prefix-only movement or transform-only byte shortening without exact seed-span wins after metadata.
Promotion trigger: A generated transform artifact reports exact selected seed spans on held-out ordinary corpora with transform metadata charged.
Stop rule: Keep transform descriptors outside .tlmr v1/v2 until exact decode and promotion gates pass.
Source artifacts: docs/TRANSFORM_VALIDATION.md, docs/MECHANISM_EXPERIMENT_RANKING.md, docs/adr/0001-transform-preconditioners.md.
forbidden_actions: No Seed Search: do not start new depth-3, depth-4, or long-span sweeps; do not claim natural-corpus compression is proven; do not claim production readiness; do not promote .tlmr v2, transform metadata, or dictionary presets to stable format support; do not weaken random, shadow, binary, or same-size control requirements; do not edit generated markdown or JSON by hand.

hypotheses.title: Residual sidecars might turn near matches into profitable exact records
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: store only residual correction data around seed-generated spans
hypotheses.best_case_argument: If generated bytes are close enough, a compact residual channel could charge correction bytes yet still beat raw literals for structured data.
hypotheses.current_evidence: whole-stream ordinary held-out negative groups=0, control negative groups=0, and current residual lanes do not prove honest full-stream savings.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Residual vector, offset, seed, checksum, and literal bytes erase every ordinary whole-stream win or controls win similarly.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: At least three ordinary whole-stream negative groups with zero comparable control wins and exact reconstruction from charged sidecar bytes.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not count residual proximity as compression unless full-stream charged records are negative and controls stay null.

hypotheses.title: Public schema dictionaries can act as decoder-public generative presets
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: decoder-public lookup tables instead of file-local raw payloads
hypotheses.best_case_argument: For structured domains, a public dictionary can be treated like a versioned preset: the file names the preset, not the raw table.
hypotheses.current_evidence: schema claim level=control_failed_on_frozen_expansion_corpora; control negative groups=2; controls currently block registry or format promotion.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Paired shadow schemas, wrong-schema dictionaries, or same-size random controls shrink with comparable density.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: Frozen public dictionaries produce held-out ordinary wins while paired shadow and binary controls remain null.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not add dictionary registry semantics to .tlmr until decoder-public identity and control separation are proven.

hypotheses.title: Recursive v2 passes may expose second-layer wins after selected replacements
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: recursive passes operate on transformed layer outputs
hypotheses.best_case_argument: Once selected records replace spans, the next byte landscape is different; v2 can test that idea while keeping layer hashes and descriptors explicit.
hypotheses.current_evidence: ordinary later-win families=0, planted offset later-win families=0, promotion_met=False.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Later-layer gains remain isolated to planted offset controls and ordinary structured fixture families do not improve.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: At least two ordinary structured fixture families show later-layer wins with exact recursive decode and no index requirement.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Keep v2 experimental; do not reinterpret v1 layer_count or emit recursive v1 outputs.

hypotheses.title: Hardware acceleration matters only after CPU evidence identifies a promoted workload
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: specialized hardware makes wider seed search practical
hypotheses.best_case_argument: If CPU streaming finds a profitable workload, GPUs or ASICs could turn archival search from theoretically possible into economically testable.
hypotheses.current_evidence: acceleration status=research-only-cpu-semantics, real GPU kernel detected=None, production blocked gates=3.
hypotheses.falsification_test: No real kernel, no CPU/GPU parity, or no CPU workload with evidence worth accelerating.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: A real kernel-backed matcher passes CPU/GPU parity on promoted workloads and improves wall-clock or energy enough to change the scale gate.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not trust GPU output or market acceleration while the GPU feature is research-only CPU semantics.

hypotheses.title: Natural-corpus viability needs repeatable non-planted selected spans
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: structured real corpora may contain enough generative spans to beat overhead
hypotheses.best_case_argument: Telomere becomes interesting only if ordinary or transformed corpora show repeatable selected spans that controls do not reproduce.
hypotheses.current_evidence: natural proof status=not_natural_corpus_proven, blocked gates=8, held-out selected spans=0, best forecast=828.0 GiB; reopen manifest status=pre_registered_reopen_manifest_only, first allowed stage=manifest-only, next compute stage=requires_human_approval_and_new_manifest_evidence; external accession status=valid_manifest_only_ready_for_human_review, entries=24, manifest_complete=True, compute_allowed=False.
hypotheses.falsification_test: Held-out prefix>=5, exact-hit, selected-span, and negative-delta gates remain null or require hundreds of GiB per expected hit.
hypotheses.promotion_trigger: NATURAL_CORPUS_PROOF_MATRIX reports qualified natural-corpus proof with ordinary selected spans or negative delta and controls still null.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Do not claim natural-corpus viability while any natural proof blocker remains.

hypotheses.title: Parallel brainstorming is useful only when every lane is bounded and falsifiable
hypotheses.whitepaper_concept: many independent ways to search the generative mechanism space
hypotheses.best_case_argument: Multiple research agents can explore different mechanism families at once if they share stop rules, source hashes, and artifact contracts.

hypotheses.promotion_trigger: New briefs propose bounded checked artifacts that improve a blocked gate without bypassing SEARCH_FRONTIER_GATE.
hypotheses.stop_rule: Use subagents for bounded audits/designs; do not launch agents that edit the same files blindly or run broad search.
